---
title: "Problem Set 3"
author: "Grayson Pettit & Elijah Roberts"
date: "2026-03-24"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  word_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r 2.f.g}
#2.f
# R Code for Part F
set.seed(123)
betas <- numeric(2000)
for(i in 1:2000) {
  W <- rnorm(1000); e <- rnorm(1000); v <- rnorm(1000); eta <- rnorm(1000)
  U <- W^2 + e
  Z <- W^2 + v
  X <- Z + W + eta
  Y <- 1*X + U
  
  # 2SLS using AER package
  fit <- AER::ivreg(Y ~ X + W | Z + W)
  betas[i] <- coef(fit)["X"]
}
mean(betas) # Outputs ~1.666
hist(betas, main="Sampling Distribution of 2SLS (Part F)")

betas <- numeric(2000)
for(i in 1:2000) {
  W <- rnorm(1000); e <- rnorm(1000); v <- rnorm(1000); eta <- rnorm(1000)
  U <- W^2 + e
  Z <- W^2 + v
  X <- Z + W + eta
  Y <- 1*X + U
  
  # 2SLS using AER package
  fit <- AER::ivreg(Y ~ X + W | Z + W)
  betas[i] <- coef(fit)["X"]
}
mean(betas) # Outputs ~1.666
hist(betas, main="Sampling Distribution of 2SLS (Part F)")


```

## Including Plots



```{r 2.g, echo=FALSE}
# 2.g

# Load the AER package for the ivreg function
# install.packages("AER") 
library(AER)

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(456)

# Initialize an empty vector to store the 2000 beta estimates
betas_g <- numeric(2000)

# Run the simulation 2,000 times
for(i in 1:2000) {
  
  # 1. Generate the i.i.d. normal variables (N = 1000)
  W   <- rnorm(1000)
  e   <- rnorm(1000)
  v   <- rnorm(1000)
  eta <- rnorm(1000)
  
  # 2. Define the DGP
  U <- W^2 + e
  Z <- W + v           # <-- The key change: Z is now linear in W
  X <- Z + W + eta
  Y <- 1 * X + U       # True causal effect (beta) is 1
  
  # 3. Estimate 2SLS (Y on X and W, instrumented by Z and W)
  fit_g <- ivreg(Y ~ X + W | Z + W)
  
  # 4. Extract and store the coefficient for X
  betas_g[i] <- coef(fit_g)["X"]
}


# Print the average of the 2,000 estimates
cat("Average 2SLS Estimate:", mean(betas_g), "\n")

# Plot the histogram to visualize the sampling distribution
hist(betas_g, 
     main = "Sampling Distribution of 2SLS (Part G)", 
     xlab = "Estimated Beta (True Beta = 1)", 
     col = "lightgreen", 
     breaks = 30)

# Add a vertical red dashed line at the true causal effect
abline(v = 1, col = "red", lwd = 3, lty = 2)


```


```{r 3.b}
#3.b

# 1. Simulate finite sample data (N = 1000)
set.seed(999)
Z <- rnorm(1000)
U <- rnorm(1000)
X <- 2*Z + U + rnorm(1000)  # X is endogenous (correlated with U)
Y <- 5*X + U                # True beta is 5

# 2. Estimate via 2SLS
library(AER)
iv_model <- ivreg(Y ~ X | Z)
beta_2SLS <- coef(iv_model)["X"]

# 3. Estimate via Control Function (CF)
first_stage <- lm(X ~ Z)
V <- resid(first_stage)     # Extract first-stage residuals
cf_model <- lm(Y ~ X + V)
beta_CF <- coef(cf_model)["X"]

# 4. Compare
cat("2SLS Estimator: ", beta_2SLS, "\n")
cat("CF Estimator:   ", beta_CF, "\n")
cat("Are they mathematically identical? ", isTRUE(all.equal(beta_2SLS, beta_CF)))

```
